title: Include monday doc content in search

posts.author: GuyLegault (Guy Legault)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would suggest that “Search everything” also search content from Monday docs.
I was trying to create an activity dashboard with certain notes to be added.
It would be imperative that the search could track those notes in an easy-to-read text format.
We found that Monday docs would suite are needs, until we realized that search everything does not actually search everything, including the content of Monday docs

posts.author: arkitrave (Eric Shepherd)
posts.cookedHtml:
There’s another longer thread on this topic. Search also doesn’t search mirrored columns, which is extremely inconvenient.

/#/t/25685

posts.author: jptcc (Juliana P)
posts.cookedHtml:
there is search function within board, and also a search function for the entire workspace. but there isn’t a search function to search within monday_docs.
monday_docs is super useful for jotting notes, meeting minutes but things could get quite lengthy overtime.
could you introduce this new feature for monday_docs please.
Thks

posts.author: Yannick (Pinta)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also super interested into the ability to do search of various or project specific Monday_Doc content.

posts.author: JJV08 (Jennifer Vogt)
posts.cookedHtml: Same! I actually came over to the community here looking for this exact topic.

posts.author: DecisiveDesign (Jason Oeltjen)
posts.cookedHtml: Why is it called “search everything” if I can’t search docs… We have been taking extensive meeting notes in docs now just went to try to find a reference to something AND SEARCH DOESN’T WORK. That basically makes docs unusable. What the hell guys?

posts.author: involuntary_admin (Arun Nayagam)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree that this is a much-needed feature. Here is a thread with more people requesting the same feature Search results include contents of Monday Docs

posts.author: Charay (Chris Ray)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great to be able to search contents of native Monday docs.

posts.author: Viewport (Julius Jeppe)
posts.cookedHtml: +1. This would be a game changer as it would allow us to drop confluence - which is the only reason we’re hanging on to attlassian as well for now. Can someone from Monday let us know if this on a roadmap and if so when can we realistically expect it?

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to add to this, right now you cannot even search a docs name and find the doc in a file column and you can only find the doc based on the name of the pulse.
PS add search to the board level as well as global, it will really help to focus results and most people know what board they want to search on

posts.author: jerricka (Jerricka Hill)
posts.cookedHtml: Here to echo the potential of workdocs as a wiki solution if they were searchable. And also able to be organized more than one folder deep, but that is a different request.

posts.author: Yannick (Pinta)
posts.cookedHtml: We completely agree. We actually changed to Monday because of the new feature of the doc and the potential to use them as a internal and external wiki. Cross doc internal content indexing and search will be a game changer in that regard. Is there any plan to implement that in the future ?

posts.author: JackiSto (Jacki Stockinger)
posts.cookedHtml: agreed - given how robust Monday’s search is in general I was shocked to discover it’s not searching workdocs. Giant bummer - crossing fingers this is in the works given this is such a new feature.

posts.author: timjasper (Tim Jasper)
posts.cookedHtml: Unusable until you can search docs. Strange omission…

posts.author: DecisiveDesign (Jason Oeltjen)
posts.cookedHtml: Why is it called “search everything” if I can’t search docs… We have been taking extensive meeting notes in docs now just went to try to find a reference to something AND SEARCH DOESN’T WORK. That basically makes docs unusable. What the heck guys?

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml: I spoke to some folks at Monday recently about workdocs limitations and was told searching is on the roadmap but no specific timeframe, sounded like it might be late in the year if anything. But fwiw, they are aware of it. Unclear why it wasn’t done in the first place other than the usual fast-growing-building-without-a-plan startup thing…

posts.author: luminos-mihaljevic (Miroslav Mihaljević)
posts.cookedHtml: Pretty important stuff, when you have millions of documents you want your team members to be able to find them easily

posts.author: Rio (Rio)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pinging the thread as well - this is SUPER important for a lot of clients. And one of those things that we expected to work!

Can the Monday group speak to this? Any updates? Plans? ETA?

posts.author: arkitrave (Eric Shepherd)
posts.cookedHtml: Search results also don’t include mirrored columns, which is extremely inconvenient as they’re a way to tie together multiple boards that share the same data. I just want Search Everything to search everything!

posts.author: mikegamm (Mike G)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to the need here. We’re ready to drop our other notetaking tool, but can’t until Monday’s workdocs are searchable.

posts.author: PaulN (Paul Nicholas)
posts.cookedHtml: Same - I’m still having to cling onto OneNote (which has excellent searching) until Monday.com add search to pickup content within Docs.

posts.author: involuntary_admin (Arun Nayagam)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for the search feature. Looks the idea (an excellent one ) was submitted more than 1.5 years ago and there have been multiple up-votes and requests to implement this feature. Can someone from Monday.com please comment on the roadmap. It makes it impossible to plan on our infrastructure roadmap without knowing if or when features will be included.

I think Workdocs has the potential to become a Notion/Confluence replacement for Wikis/knoweldge management with a few key features added. I would rate content-search as the #1 feature that is missing.

posts.author: jmorris (Joseph Morris)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature should be baked in by default. As someone who is looking to utilize Monday as a platform to remove the 30 apps/services currently in use this feature is almost a necessity. I have a user who is specifically trying to get rid of OneNote/Evernote by using Monday Docs, and due to the lack of search they can’t utilize it for meeting notes that can easily be searched.

posts.author: mauriziodelmonte (maurizio)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is completely unexpected from a user perspective: what’s the reason for not to being able to find your own documents?
It is quite a basic use case for people using tools like monday to automate workslows.

posts.author: amellman (Aaron Mellman)
posts.cookedHtml: How is this not baked in? I’m absolutely amazed that this has not been added as a feature from the get-go, it would make Monday.com so much more useful.

posts.author: homeplay (James Ratcliffe)
posts.cookedHtml: Just chiming in again to see if this is on the road map yet? Thanks!

posts.author: kubster96 (Jakub Jungiewicz)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, does anybody have any update on this one? We are currently deciding what documentation tool to use.

posts.author: mbillep (Mario Billep)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, is there anything new? We are new users and can not believe that this “function” is still missing.

posts.author: evanovich (Evan)
posts.cookedHtml: How is this not a thing, still? Coming up on two years and we still can’t search within our workdocs? Monday.com people - I know you can do better! Why even offer this functionality to begin with if it isn’t searchable? Also makes search everywhere very misleading. Just my 5 cents.

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 Without we can’t use it as an internal doc

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
Throwing my vote in for this one too.
On big projects with lots of docs all linked to a board, this is pretty vital when trying to find a piece of information. We’ll have to keep the important stuff in OneNote until this is improved.

posts.author: dara_t (Dara Thomason)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this. Need to be able to search Monday Docs.

posts.author: b.quirion (B. Quirion)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an essential feature for us. We’ve used Monday to track our projects for a few months now and that is fine. The problem is that we recently started to track our software bugs in Monday as well, but the only way to add information like pictures, tracebacks, lots of rich text is through documents. But if we want to search through an old traceback or old issue to know if an issue is related or the same one, we just can’t now that we’re on Monday. Having the ability to search through all documents would help a lot, even if it is just a checkbox to check when searching and that it takes a few minutes to search. It would be way better than nothing.

posts.author: GarethD (Gareth Doutch)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking to implement a knowledge base.

There’s no point in using Monday.com unless articles turn up when you search.

It would be unusable otherwise.

posts.author: evanovich (Evan)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT help! Has this had any traction with your product teams?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone!

Thanks for your patience.

I hear you all and have checked in with the relevant product team - I can let you know that whilst subject to change, this is a feature we are planning to release in H1 this year. I’ve checked in incase there’s any more information I can share and will update you once I hear back. Thank you for your patience

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: https://monday.slack.com/archives/C023GQWGZ51/p1705964834824009?thread_ts=1700484186.371729&cid=C023GQWGZ51

posts.author: shoebox (ShoeBox)
posts.cookedHtml: we need this urgently. docs just don’t make much sense if not searchable.

posts.author: Serhii (Serhii Rostovskyi)
posts.cookedHtml: Docs functionality is great opportunity of Knowledge Base. But Search functionality discovers only Doc’s title. Search engine has to include Monday Doc content to be usable for Knowledge Base purposes.

posts.author: pkk (PK)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT could you provide any updates to this feature request, please?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @pkk,

At this time, I don’t have any new updates to share since my last comment. Our team is working on refining some aspects of the feature and its release so I will be sure to communicate these changes when they’re confirmed

posts.author: TomD3 (Tom Acheson-Gray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

Also really would like to see this feature so that we can build an internal wiki for our organisation. Monday has loads of great features but this seems like a massive oversight!

@BiancaT If you need any further feedback to get this bumped up a dev queue let me know. Also if you have an updated ETA on release that would be great?

Thanks!

posts.author: ProductivityApps (Travis C)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @BiancaT , just wondering if this Search Workdocs feature the team is working on will also have a “replace” function?

Eg. I search for “Apple” in all of my documents and replace that phrase with “Google”.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Checking in with the team - great idea @ProductivityApps

@TomD3 I will also request whether we have an approved timeline to share for you! At this stage, I am not sure that we do but of course I will try my best to get some more insights from our product team

posts.author: quikbox (Nick Rodriguez)
posts.cookedHtml: Search Everything should mean search everything. I have to keep create and keep documents in 2 different spots so i can find them by content. Other products like NextCloud and sharepoint can search the content of documents, including wiki like documents. This kind of technology has been around for years, we need it in Monday.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ProductivityApps,

Thanks for your patience with me! The team have mentioned that search and replace is a feature they’re considering however no confirmed timeline just yet. I will keep an eye on it!

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml:
Love the suggestion for search and replace.

@BiancaT any updates on the timeline for the search docs feature?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sam,

At this stage we don’t have any update to share despite the fact that development is being considered for the future. I am sorry I am unable to provide further clarity at the moment - thanks for your understanding!

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml: Bianca, thanks for the quick response. From an earlier comment, I thought the search feature on documents was on the roadmap for release in Q1. Since it’s almost the end of Q1 I am guessing that won’t happen, but wondering if it’s coming in Q2?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sam,

If you’re referring to my initial comment, this plan is to have this feature released during H1 however please let me know if I’ve missed a comment! My apologies if there was any confusion caused here.

posts.author: bradhasha (Brad Hasha)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca - if the plan is H1 surely there is a development timeline at this point since it is now Q2. Otherwise confidence this is going to happen is very low. The ROI for Monday as a business is quite significant with this feature addition as I can assure you this is hindering adoption of the your platform by more than just my team.

To echo others here, this is a huge oversight in functionality highlighted by my team as well. It is embarrassing to try to explain the lack of such a crucial feature in a system made for managing work and content.

To tack on, this is not the first instance to where the platform has disappointed by lacking what most would consider crucial features. All of which I have found to be existing open threads with no traction from your product team. All are of full of comments acknowledging your users but then no actual prioritization happens. Some of the threads have been open for multiple years. That is not an acceptable way to treat your paying customers. Prioritize the features we’re asking you for, nail down a timeline, share it, and do the work. It isn’t that complicated; my team does it everyday for our customers with our product.

posts.author: KSegWin (Kevin Seguin [Monday Ambassador])
posts.cookedHtml: With Bianca gone (sadness) I wonder if @Julietteb has an update on a timeline for this?

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml: No updates yet on the timing for this request?

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, It would be nice if Monday.com implements a feature to allow one to filter the contents of the files stored on all Monday’s boards in the search everything section. I would like to know if it is on Monday´s plans to implement such feature. For now my Company relies on Dropbox to make this type of content search.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @agranville

Thanks for the suggestion! Are you talking about locating a file based on the content inside the file itself rather than just the name of the file?

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Julia! Thanks for your reply

Yes exactly. We would like to be able to locate documents (pdf, word) based on their contents.
One of our uses of Monday is to manage our projects that involves storing lots of different types of documents, such as contracts, reports and invoices. Very often, when we need to prepare a new report, we first make a search on our documents repository in order to find any other similar report, so we can save time. Currently, we are relying on Dropbox seach feature, but we would like to have it on Monday so we could use a one tool do all

posts.author: lyanchev (Lyubomir Yanchev)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also find this feature extremely useful! It can definitely bring Monday.com closer to your vision of one tool to rule all!

I’d also include .csv to the list of files I can search within.

posts.author: Yannick (Pinta)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also interested in this feature. At least, if not in the document linked/hosted on Monday.com, we should be able to search within Monday.com new Doc feature.

posts.author: Cketchum (Charlie Ketchum)
posts.cookedHtml: I am currently looking for a way to do this myself. Has there been any updates added that allows for a file content search?

posts.author: kathy-qcb (Kathy Cunningham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are there any updates on this feature request? This is an issue for our organization as well.

We have embraced “monday Workdocs” as a native solution for multiple types of longer-form text. I had assumed we would be able to search the file contents, but based on my attempts today, it would seem this is not possible.

For example, today I needed to search through several months of meeting minutes for a specific documentation request. Sadly, the search tool cannot find terms that I can literally see in the files when they are open.

What are the current plans for this feature? Especially for the native Workdocs, this seems like something that monday should be able to wrangle. It is definitely needed!

posts.author: D.Pollitt (David Pollitt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Kathy!
I’ve been running into the same need. I have found an app called AI Copilot in the app marketplace that says if can read PDFs. I am about to start working with it soon. From the example I’ve seen, it should be able to extract information into a text box and then it becomes searchable once it’s text.
I know this doesn’t exactly help but maybe it’s worth checking out to see if it can get you closer to your goal?

posts.author: kathy-qcb (Kathy Cunningham)
posts.cookedHtml: @D.Pollitt Thanks for the recommendation. I will check this out!

posts.author: dara_t (Dara Thomason)
posts.cookedHtml: Would like to be able to search for Monday docs that aren’t templates in a board - not the general search functionality.

posts.author: shoebox (ShoeBox)
posts.cookedHtml: is this a duplicate of Search everything to include monday doc content as well - Feature requests - monday Community Forum ?

posts.author: marcusdiddle (Marcus Diddle)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m only learning now that the extensive meeting notes I’ve been taking in the form of Docs within Monday aren’t searchable. This entirely negates my use of Monday. As much as I hate to do it, I’ll effectively be forced to transfer all of my notes to OneNote where at least I know I’ll be able to search across my notes for specific terms.

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb any updates?

posts.author: mondayguru (Cole Casper)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also looking to export all my notes from monday.com to another tool and considering switching my team to confluence.

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml: Crazy that after all these years, any content you write in a Doc in Monday is STILL not searchable using the “Search Everything” or Cmd + B bolt bar. I posted on this thread years ago after a conversation with a product manager at Monday who said it was coming soon. That was cerca 2022. Still in H2 2024 nothing. Seems like they’ve given up on Docs completely.

posts.author: dallaslightner (dlighted)
posts.cookedHtml: Please, for the love of God - we need this feature. This will put notion out of business. The search function needs to be able to search through docs. We need to be able to build internal Wikis without weird workarounds. CMD F on a board at the bare minimum. Gold star if we are able to search through all docs in a workspace using CMD B. PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE!

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb any updates? this is a feature that is crucial.

posts.author: manishaghosh (MG)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, @GuyLegault this app does what you’re looking for : Doc Search Pro - monday.com App Marketplace

posts.author: daniel.s (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this feature?

posts.author: dallaslightner (dlighted)
posts.cookedHtml: Notion is not going out of business fyi.

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s nice that someone has taken the time to build an app to overcome Monday’s limitations, but we should not have to pay an additional $12/month for a feature that should have been built into Monday from the start.
Monday’s Dev Team has really lost sight of what the consumers want. They seem to spend time on features nobody asked for and ignore the most voted requests from the Community.

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml: Our company is removing all of our process documentation from Monday Workdocs due to Monday lacking this search feature. We are moving everything to HelpJuice which is fully searchable.

posts.author: manishaghosh (MG)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Charlie, this is my app. I understand your concern. Would you like to try the app? Otherwise, if you have any specific request for the app, would you be open to talk?

posts.author: Atul (Atul | cloudvandana apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed. This feature would be great to have.

@charlieg you can also move them to Google Docs which would be free if you guys already use google workspace.
Just a thought.

posts.author: ktraverso (Kellie Traverso)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, adding my vote to this request. This feature is needed!

posts.author: jwang (JJ)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT, hello, this would be very helpful for our organization as well. Just wanted to see if there is an any update on this bring on the roadmap for this year.

posts.author: patkoscsaba (Patkós Csaba)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am adding my voice to the crowd. This feature would help us as well a lot. We are planning migrating fully to Monday … including from our Confluence wikis … lots of docs.
I also tried the app linked above “Doc Search Pro”, but it is not available on the Monday marketplace any more and if I instlall it from the link I cannot add it get the “Workspace apps” section to show it, so not even that can help at the moment.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team We want you to know that we’ve heard your feedback, searching monday docs through “Search everything” has been a popular request, and we truly appreciate your thoughts on this.

This is officially on our long-term roadmap! While we don’t have a release date just yet, our team is committed to bringing this feature to life. We will continue to update you as progress is made! Thank you again for helping shape the future of monday.com, we’re always here to listen and learn!

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: and some how you are happy to pay 200/month for it, am I missing something?

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml: We already use HelpJuice to maintain an external FAQ repository for customers. It was not an additional expense to move Internal documents over to a platform we were already using for something else.

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: Interesting thanks for sharing. I was just trying to understand the users mindset here because recently I made an app that searches emails and activities but there is no traction on it. So I am assuming that searching is a problem for users but not big enough to pay for it

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Recently I made an app that can search through your Emails and Activities of your CRM

but some of my users also asked for searching through file would be cool an app I am making one anyone interested in beta testing it?

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! I’m excited to announce that you will soon be able to search for Doc content via the Search Everything icon! This feature is coming very soon. We will update you all once this feature has been released. Thank you for all of your input–it has shaped our platform for the better!

posts.author: JanieW (Janie Walton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Shannon,

I have just tried to use this feature, but it doesn’t achieve what I was hoping for. I want to search within the doc but used the Search Everything as this seems to be the only search available. The search returned the right document but when I click on the result, it only takes me to the start of the document and not to the particular word in the doc. So this isn’t ‘Solved’ for me. What are the plans for a Find feature within a document, similar to the one available in the boards ?

Thanks,

Janie

posts.author: Atul (Atul | cloudvandana apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think this feature will be out “soon“!

@shannon-cxmonday please inform us once it is generally available.

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I’m excited to share that this feature has been released! Search now picks up content from Monday docs, so any notes you add will actually show up when you look for them. This should save you from having to dig around or wonder where you saved something.

Thanks for speaking up about this. Feedback like yours is what helps us get the details right. If you have had a chance to test it out, let me know how it is working for you!
